text:
There once was a young man named Felix. Ever since he could remember, he felt different. He didn’t understand why. He tried everything. He volunteered at a shelter. He volunteered on farms. He even joined scouts.

But it wasn't enough. He felt like there was always something unsatisfactory about his life.

Then one evening, he realized it: He hated his job. Every day was the same. The humdrum. The monotony.

One day, after a particularly excruciating shift at the pet grooming salon where he dyed endless coats in pastels, he decided enough was enough.

He packed his bag with scissors, brushes, and clips. He walked out the door.

It didn't take long.

He went to an abandoned building. He started pulling at the boarded-up windows. Finally, one broke.

He jumped inside, grabbed his tools from his bag, and began hacking at the wood.

Hours turned into nights.

Finally, the room was wide open.
